task_summary: Prepared the engineering brief in [prep.json](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/define-a-robust-mobile-and-20260410-0143/prep.json). The brief concludes this does need codebase investigation because the timeline interaction stack is spread across multiple files, current behavior is desktop-first, and there are existing mobile/touch patterns elsewhere in the repo that should shape the strategy.

key_evidence.point: Video editor mobile support currently exists at the shell/layout level, not at the timeline interaction level.
Timeline drag/resize behavior is coupled to shared interaction-state gating for save/poll conflict avoidance.
There are reusable touch patterns already in the repo for tap-vs-scroll thresholds and touch-friendly trim handles.

relevant_code.why: Top-level timeline orchestrator for drag, marquee select, pinned groups, and playhead handler wiring.
Owns the timeline surface, resize lifecycle, playhead, shot-group overlays, and edit-area pointer entrypoint.
Implements current drag semantics for single clips, multi-select, pinned groups, cross-track moves, and new-track creation.
Unified resize/trim commit path for free clips and pinned groups.
Defers saves during active drag/resize gestures and flushes when interaction ends.

test_expectations.what_it_checks: Save deferral while drag/resize is active.
Drag thresholds, multi-drag, and pinned-group drag semantics.
Resize lifecycle, playhead sizing, and pinned-group canvas behavior.

constraints: Do not break desktop pointer and keyboard workflows.
Keep pinned shot groups first-class across touch interactions.
Reuse existing playback sync paths instead of introducing parallel playhead logic.
Avoid hover-only and right-click-only core actions on touch.
suggested_approach: Use the brief at the output path as the primary planning context. It recommends an explicit interaction state model, device-specific gesture mapping for phone vs iPad, phased rollout from selection/playhead/inspector to move/trim/track ops, and targeted touch regression tests grounded in the existing drag/resize/persistence architecture.
